description: Supply must equal demand for each carrier at each node and time step
shadow_price_interpretation: Marginal cost of meeting one additional GWh of demand at that node/time
notes: Core constraint of every energy model; shadow price = locational marginal price (LMP)

description: Output flow <= capacity * max_load; links dispatch to installed capacity
shadow_price_interpretation: Value of 1 GW more conversion capacity (operational bound only)
notes: Different from capacity_limit — this is the operational capacity factor constraint

description: Flow limited to installed capacity times availability factor at each time step
notes: Binds when technology runs at full capacity

description: Charge/discharge flow limited to power capacity
Transport flow limited to installed transport capacity
notes: Interconnector utilization constraint

description: Technology must produce at least a minimum number of full-load hours per year
notes: Used for must-run or baseload technologies

description: Storage level at next time step = current level + charge - discharge (energy balance)
notes: Time-linking constraint for storage

description: Storage level cannot exceed installed energy capacity
Spillage limited to current storage level
Charge flow only when binary = 1 (no simultaneous charge and discharge)
notes: Only active if binary storage formulation used

description: Discharge flow only when binary = 0
notes: Only active if binary storage formulation used

description: Output flow = input flow * (1 - loss_factor * distance)
notes: Models resistive/friction losses on pipelines and power lines

description: Retrofit technology output coupled to base technology operation
notes: Links CCS retrofit dispatch to host plant dispatch

description: Helper variable linking on/off binary to capacity
notes: Only active for on/off (binary) technologies

description: Lower bound on capacity when technology is on
Upper bound on capacity when technology is on
Minimum load when technology is on (min-load constraint)
Maximum load when technology is on (max-load constraint)
Shed demand limited to max_shed_demand fraction of total demand
notes: Prevents unbounded unmet demand

description: Capacity in year y = existing + sum of all capacity_additions within depreciation period
shadow_price_interpretation: Shadow price = value of 1 GW free capacity for that tech/node/year
notes: Key constraint for RC dual-based computation

description: Accounts for capacity additions made before the model horizon
notes: Handles pre-existing investments whose lifetime extends into horizon

description: Delays capacity availability by construction_time years
Handles construction decisions made before model start
Equality: capacity_addition = capacity_approximation (linearization helper)
notes: Part of piecewise-linear CAPEX approximation

description: Equality: cost_capex_overnight = capex_approximation
notes: Part of piecewise-linear CAPEX approximation

description: cost_capex_overnight = capex_specific * capacity_addition
notes: Simple linear CAPEX; used when no piecewise approximation

description: Piecewise-linear CAPEX approximation for economies of scale
notes: Active only if pwa_capex enabled

description: CAPEX for storage = power_capex * power_capacity + energy_capex * energy_capacity
notes: Separate power and energy cost terms for storage

description: CAPEX for transport = capex_per_distance * distance * capacity
notes: Distance-weighted investment cost

description: Annualizes overnight CAPEX: cost_capex_yearly = annuity_factor * cost_capex_overnight
notes: Converts lump-sum to annual payment

description: Sums capex_yearly over all technologies to total
notes: Aggregation constraint

description: cost_opex_yearly = fixed_opex * capacity + variable_opex * flow
Sums opex_yearly over all technologies
Cost of carrier imports = flow * carrier_price
Sums carrier costs over all carriers and nodes
Cost of unmet demand = shed_demand * price_shed_demand
notes: High penalty price discourages shedding

primary_variables: cost_total, cost_capex_yearly_total, cost_opex_yearly_total, cost_carrier_total, cost_shed_demand, cost_carbon
description: cost_total = sum of all cost components
Discounts annual cost to present value
notes: Objective function contribution

description: Variable OPEX for conversion = sum(flow * opex_var) over time steps
Variable OPEX for storage
Variable OPEX for transport
Carbon cost = emissions * carbon_price (if price_carbon > 0)
notes: Only relevant if explicit carbon price is set

description: Emissions from conversion = flow * carbon_intensity
Emissions from storage operations
notes: Usually negligible

description: Emissions from transport (e.g., gas leakage)
Sums emissions across all technologies
Emissions from imported carriers (e.g., imported gas)
Sums carrier emissions
Total annual emissions = tech emissions + carrier emissions
notes: Tracking constraint; shadow price = effective carbon price if limit binds

description: Cumulative emissions = sum of annual emissions over years
Annual emissions <= annual limit (e.g., net-zero in 2050)
shadow_price_interpretation: Euro/tCO2 — marginal cost of tightening the annual cap by 1 tonne
notes: In Crystal Ball: limit=0 in 2050 only; unconstrained in other years unless budget binds

description: Penalty variable for exceeding annual limit; overshoot * 5000 Euro/tCO2 added to cost
notes: Soft constraint; shadow price reflects penalty price

description: Cumulative emissions <= 23.15 Gt CO2 over 2022-2050
shadow_price_interpretation: Euro/tCO2 — marginal cost of tightening cumulative budget by 1 tonne
notes: Most binding CO2 constraint in Crystal Ball; drives technology choice across all years

description: Penalty for exceeding cumulative budget; 5000 Euro/tCO2
notes: Soft constraint

description: Limits new investment to remaining headroom below capacity limit
shadow_price_interpretation: Euro/GW — value of 1 GW more installable potential (e.g., more wind area)
notes: Binding in regions with scarce land/resource; wind_onshore most relevant in Crystal Ball

description: Prevents any further addition once total capacity hits the limit
notes: Hard stop; shadow price can be very large

description: Minimum capacity addition when technology is built (lumpiness constraint)
notes: Boundary only if set to non-zero; reflects minimum viable plant size

description: Maximum capacity addition per year per technology
notes: Limits build rate; related to supply chain constraints

description: Hourly import of carrier from outside system <= availability limit
shadow_price_interpretation: Euro/GWh — marginal value of additional import capacity
notes: Biomass import limits are key in Crystal Ball; also LNG

description: Hourly export of carrier outside system <= availability limit
notes: Shadow price = value of additional export capacity

description: Annual import of carrier <= yearly availability limit
notes: More aggregate than hourly constraint

description: Annual export <= yearly limit
Capacity addition in year y <= diffusion_rate * (existing_capacity + market_share_base)
shadow_price_interpretation: Euro/GW — value of relaxing the deployment speed limit
notes: Limits how fast a technology can scale; reflects supply chain and learning constraints

description: Global version of diffusion limit across all nodes
notes: Aggregate deployment growth cap

description: Energy capacity >= min_ratio * power capacity
notes: Design constraint for storage technology sizing

description: Energy capacity <= max_ratio * power capacity
